channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: National Trust SA faces inquiry motion amid $230,000 property dispute
item.description: Another National of Trust South Australia branch goes public about its financial dispute with the high-profile heritage group, as the state opposition announces it will refer members' concerns to a parliamentary committee.
item.dc:creator: George Yankovich and Samuel O'Brien

media:group.media:description: Barry Long at the Millicent Museum.

item.title: Woman dies after being hit by ute on Pacific Motorway
item.description: Police say initial enquiries indicate a female was out of her vehicle when she was struck.

media:group.media:description: Any witnesses to a multi-car crash on the Pacific Motorway are urged to contact police.

item.title: Granny flat ban lifted for some residents under airport flight path
item.description: The NSW government will permit secondary dwellings such as granny flats in some areas affected by noise from Western Sydney International Airport.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Chandler

media:group.media:description: Secondary dwellings were banned in LGAs such as Wollondilly and Penrith to mitigate noise impacts and population growth.

item.title: Behind the front lines of the war against illegal waste
item.description: While Queensland councils cleaned up enough illegally dumped waste to fill more than 1,200 garbage trucks, investigators say they are getting better at catching those doing the wrong thing.
item.dc:creator: Charlie McLean

media:group.media:description: Mark Bolton investigates reports of illegal waste dumping in Ipswich.

item.title: The dead fish waste disaster that Tasmania failed to plan for
item.description: High-volume salmon mortalities last year swamped Tasmania's waste streams as companies scrambled to find somewhere to accept them, documents show. There are now concerns the state remains unprepared for future mass deaths.
item.dc:creator: Adam Holmes

media:group.media:description: Salmon in bins, prior to being processed or disposed of during the mortality event.

item.title: A secret mouse experiment in the 1990s threatened Brisbane's water supply
item.description: Brisbane was days away from running out of drinkable water in the 1990s due to a secret mouse experiment that was kept hidden from the public … until now.
item.dc:creator: Craig Zonca and Loretta Ryan and Kenji Sato

media:group.media:description: Jim Soorley says the secret was kept strictly under wraps.

item.title: Breaking: Trump says he has postponed a planned new strike on Iran
item.description: US President Donald Trump says an attack on Iran that was "scheduled for tomorrow" has been postponed at the request of gulf state leaders.
item.dc:creator: Brad Ryan

media:group.media:description: Donald Trump has been trying to extract an acceptable "deal" from Iran for weeks.

item.title: It's time for Eric Abetz to tackle Tasmania's debt. How far will he go?
item.description: The lead-up to state budget day is normally a time for flashy funding announcements. But as Tasmania stares down the barrel of $7 billion of debt, things are looking a bit bleaker.
item.dc:creator: Josh Duggan

media:group.media:description: This year will be Eric Abetz's first major budget after only small tweaks post-election last year.

item.title: See where climate change is hitting Victoria's infrastructure hardest
item.description: At least $57 billion worth of infrastructure in Victoria is vulnerable to more frequent and severe bushfires, floods and extreme heat. This figure is expected to grow.
item.dc:creator: Alex Lim and Sacha Payne

item.title: 'Two years to live': Why we used our super for weight loss surgery
item.description: Weight loss surgery has become an increasingly common option for people grappling with obesity — and many are funding it using retirement savings.
item.dc:creator: Johnson Raela

media:group.media:description: Fili Fili and Natasha Narayan-Fili both had weight loss surgery in 2025.

item.title: 'The party had to end': Young investors torn on budget tax reform
item.description: Some young Australians locked out of the property market due to unaffordable housing have pursued ETFs and "rentvesting" as a way to build financial security. Here's what they make of the budget's CGT reforms.
item.dc:creator: Lin Lin

media:group.media:description: Young ETF investor Vanessa says the budget is a "step in the right direction" but expects to take a financial hit.

item.title: Should a nurse pay same tax rate as their employer? The budget says no
item.description: As the federal budget prompts howls of protest from those who earn the bulk of their income from owning assets rather than working, we shouldn't forget the relative privilege that allowed many of those people to take those risks.
item.dc:creator: Michael Janda

media:group.media:description: It's easier to take a gamble on an investment if you are already privileged.

item.title: Two German film students track down their stolen movie in new doco
item.description: What do you do when your stolen film wins a slew of awards for the thief? In the case of these filmmakers, you travel across the globe to hunt him down.

media:group.media:description: After their film was stolen, Julius Drost and Moritz Henneberg immediately saw the cinematic potential of their story.

item.title: Rising costs can put pressure on a relationship. Here's how to address it
item.description: While financial tension between partners isn't always about money, many people clash over values, spending habits, and how they respond to financial pressure.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Scott

media:group.media:description: Rising interest rates and fuel prices are causing families to rethink spending.

item.title: 'I wish I were dead': The heartbreaking toll of Australia's favourite pastime
item.description: Australians are the world's biggest gamblers, losing billions every year. But the true cost goes far beyond the financial.
item.dc:creator: Anna Levy

media:group.media:description: Gambling is often considered a national pastime in Australia.

item.title: Quiz: Guess the correct order of famous movie quotes from the 2000s
item.description: The ABC Timelines quiz is back to test your knowledge of some of the most iconic movie quotes of the 2000s.

item.title: Elon Musk loses lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman
item.description: The Tesla boss accused OpenAI of wrongfully trying to enrich investors and insiders at the nonprofit's expense, and failing to prioritise AI's safety.

media:group.media:description: Elon Musk (right) launched the lawsuit against Sam Altman, OpenAI and Microsoft.

item.title: Two men arrested over stunt at Punch the monkey's enclosure
item.description: One of the men was detained by a zookeeper and arrested after allegedly climbing a fence and breaking into the enclosure.

media:group.media:description: Baby Japanese macaque Punch went viral earlier this year for his obsession with a stuffed orangutan.

item.title: Journalist in London stabbed at behest of Iran, court told
item.description: Pouria Zaratifoukolaei, who works for a Persian-language outlet critical of the Iranian government, was attacked near his home in London in 2024.

media:group.media:description: Romanian nationals Nandito Badea and George Stana are accused of stabbing the Iran International journalist Pouria Zeraati near his home in Wimbledon in March 2024.

item.title: Spanish PM's Socialists party on notice after historic defeat in stronghold
item.description: Voters in the region of Andalusia have sent the ruling Spain's Socialists yet another warning, adding to concerns Pedro Sánchez's government could suffer an electoral wipe-out in a nationwide vote.

media:group.media:description: The Socialist party of Pedro Sánchez has suffered multiple electoral setbacks in recent months.

item.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship docks for disinfection
item.description: The MV Hondius and its crew has docked at the port of Rotterdam in the Netherlands.

media:group.media:description: A person wearing protective equipment throws bedding next to the cruise ship MV Hondius, where authorities are preparing quarantine arrangements.

item.title: Man charged with murder after woman and two children found dead in Sydney home
item.description: A man, 47, has been charged after three people were found dead in a home in Sydney's south-west in what police allege is a domestic violence-related incident.

media:group.media:description: Police say no firearms were found at the home.

item.title: Stalking accusations against WA police assistant commissioner, trial begins
item.description: The court hears WA police assistant commissioner Gailene Hamilton allegedly made the complainant — a subordinate officer — feel "smothered" and used a restricted access computer system to check on her location.
item.dc:creator: David Weber

media:group.media:description: Gailene Hamilton's trial in Perth begins over alleged stalking.

item.title: Bodies of four missing Italian scuba divers recovered in underwater cave in Maldives
item.description: The bodies of four missing Italian scuba divers who died last week have been found in an underwater cave in the Maldives.

media:group.media:description: Monica Montefalcone (left) and daughter Giorgia Sommacal were among the five divers.

item.title: NT police officer on trial for allegedly attempting to pervert course of justice
item.description: A jury has heard police officer Karol Jarentowski repeatedly attempted to pressure his former partner into dropping aggravated assault charges against him, including at one point threatening her child support.
item.dc:creator: Roxanne Fitzgerald

media:group.media:description: Karol Jarentowski (centre) is on trial in the NT Supreme Court and has pleaded not guilty.
